LLMCadenceOverride.description:
Per-cadence model override.

Monthly and annual cadences benefit from higher-reasoning research-grade
models (e.g. o3 with extended thinking).

Traces: SRC-032 (annual high-reasoning), SRC-054 (configurable research LLM)

YAML example::

    cadence_overrides:
      monthly:
        model: o3
        thinking: false
      annual:
        model: o3
        thinking: true

model.description: Model name for this cadence override. Examples: 'o3', 'claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219'.
model.title: Model

thinking.description: Enable extended thinking / reasoning mode (e.g. o3 extended thinking). Recommended: true for annual synthesis only (SRC-032).
thinking.title: Thinking

LLMCadenceOverride.title: LLMCadenceOverride

LLMConfig.description:
LLM provider and model configuration.

Traces: SRC-055–SRC-061 (provider-agnostic LLM layer),
        SRC-057 (OpenAI as default provider)

YAML example::

    llm:
      provider: openai
      model: gpt-4o
      cadence_overrides:
        monthly:
          model: o3
          thinking: false
        annual:
          model: o3
          thinking: true

provider.description: LLM provider. Supported: 'openai' (default), 'anthropic'. Provider swap requires only this field change (SRC-056).
provider.title: Provider

model.description: Default model name for daily and weekly curation. OpenAI examples: 'gpt-4o', 'o3-mini'. Anthropic examples: 'claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219'.
model.title: Model

cadence_overrides.description: Per-cadence model overrides. Keys must be one of: 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly', 'annual'. Monthly and annual commonly use a more capable research model (SRC-054).
cadence_overrides.title: Cadence Overrides

LLMConfig.title: LLMConfig

LimitsConfig.description:
Top-N article limits per cadence.

Controls how many items the LLM is asked to select from the candidate pool.
These are soft targets — the LLM may return fewer if quality thresholds
are not met.

Traces: SRC-029 (daily), SRC-030 (weekly), SRC-031 (monthly), SRC-032 (annual)

YAML example::

    limits:
      daily_top_n:   10
      weekly_top_n:  7
      monthly_top_n: 10
      annual_top_n:  10

daily_top_n.description: Maximum articles to select for a daily digest. Recommended: 5–15 (SRC-029).
daily_top_n.title: Daily Top N

weekly_top_n.description: Maximum articles to select for a weekly digest. Recommended: 5–10 (SRC-030).
weekly_top_n.title: Weekly Top N

monthly_top_n.description: Maximum articles to select for a monthly digest. Recommended: 8–15 (SRC-031).
monthly_top_n.title: Monthly Top N

annual_top_n.description: Top articles + predictions count for the annual digest. Spec requires exactly 10 articles + 10 predictions (SRC-032). Range: 1–20.
annual_top_n.title: Annual Top N

LimitsConfig.title: LimitsConfig

SourcesConfig.description:
Source tier configuration.

Five tiers map to the architecture's source hierarchy (SRC-016–SRC-021).
All tiers are optional lists — the sourcing agent uses these as allowlists
when ranking and filtering web search results.

Traces: SRC-016 (tier hierarchy), SRC-017 (Tier 1a — user custom),
        SRC-018 (Tier 1b — business press), SRC-019 (Tier 2 — AI blogs),
        SRC-020 (Tier 3 — tech business press), SRC-021 (Tier 4 — policy/research),
        SRC-034 (user-configurable sources)

YAML example::

    sources:
      custom:   []               # Tier 1a (optional)
      tier_1b:
        - reuters.com
        - bloomberg.com
      tier_2:
        - openai.com
        - anthropic.com
      tier_3:
        - techcrunch.com
      tier_4:
        - brookings.edu

custom.description: Tier 1a — user-specified priority sources (SRC-017). Domain strings only (e.g. 'myblog.example.com'). These are treated with highest priority during curation scoring.
custom.title: Custom

tier_1b.description: Tier 1b — popular business press (SRC-018). Examples: reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, economist.com.
tier_1b.title: Tier 1B

tier_2.description: Tier 2 — top AI and tech company blogs (SRC-019). Examples: openai.com, anthropic.com, huggingface.co, news.ycombinator.com.
tier_2.title: Tier 2

tier_3.description: Tier 3 — tech business press (SRC-020). Examples: techcrunch.com, theverge.com, technologyreview.com, wired.com.
tier_3.title: Tier 3

tier_4.description: Tier 4 — policy and research institutions (SRC-021). Examples: brookings.edu, rand.org, hai.stanford.edu, ainowresearch.org.
tier_4.title: Tier 4

SourcesConfig.title: SourcesConfig

TwitterConfig.description:
Twitter/X sourcing configuration.

When ``enabled: false``, the sourcing agent skips Twitter entirely and
logs a note. The digest is still produced from web sources (SRC-148).

Traces: SRC-036–SRC-046 (configurable influencer list),
        SRC-047 (signal role — not primary news),
        SRC-148 (graceful degradation when unavailable)

YAML example::

    twitter:
      enabled: true
      handles:
        - { handle: karpathy, weight: 1.0 }
        - { handle: sama,     weight: 1.0 }

enabled.description: Set false to skip Twitter/X entirely for this agent. The digest is still produced from web sources (SRC-148).
enabled.title: Enabled

handles.description: Influencer handles to monitor. Add, remove, or re-weight without code changes (SRC-046). If empty and enabled=true, no Twitter signal is collected.
handles.title: Handles

TwitterConfig.title: TwitterConfig

TwitterHandleConfig.description:
A single influencer handle with optional signal weight.

Handles can be added, removed, or re-weighted without code changes (SRC-046).
Twitter content is signal only — not primary news (SRC-047).

Traces: SRC-036–SRC-046 (configurable influencer list), SRC-047 (signal role)

YAML example::

    - handle: karpathy
      weight: 1.5

handle.description: Twitter/X handle without the @ prefix. Examples: 'karpathy', 'sama', 'ylecun'.
handle.title: Handle

weight.description: Signal weight for this handle (0.0 < weight ≤ 10.0). Higher weight = more prominent in the LLM prompt's influencer-signal section. Default 1.0. Technical/research agents may boost specialist handles (SRC-046).
weight.title: Weight

TwitterHandleConfig.title: TwitterHandleConfig

description: JSON Schema for a per-agent YAML configuration file. Validated by load_agent_config() at startup. Secrets (API keys, tokens) must NEVER appear in this file — use env vars. Requirement traces: SRC-017–SRC-021, SRC-029–SRC-032, SRC-036–SRC-046, SRC-054, SRC-057, SRC-071–SRC-073, SRC-113, SRC-129, SRC-145.

agent_id.description: Unique agent identifier. Used as the storage namespace and output directory component. Must be a valid slug: letters, digits, hyphens, underscores only.
agent_id.title: Agent Id

curation_prompt.description: Path to the base curation prompt template directory or a specific file. When a directory is given, the appropriate cadence file (daily.md / weekly.md / monthly.md / annual.md) is selected at runtime. Prompt SHA-256 is recorded in all digest outputs (SRC-113, SRC-129).
curation_prompt.title: Curation Prompt

output_dir.description: Base output directory for digest files. The placeholder ``{agent_id}`` is resolved to the agent's ``agent_id`` at load time (SRC-145). Output file pattern: ``{output_dir}/YYYY-MM-DD-{cadence}.{md|html|json}``.
output_dir.title: Output Dir

title: AI News Agent — Per-Agent Configuration Schema
